transcript: Oh, hi. Yes, I’m Buster Cheeks. Uh, I was lookin’ for an apartment. I was wonderin’ if you could help me, please—
Um. What size apartment are you looking for? Hello?
Hi, there! Can you hear me?
I—I—yeah, I have a bad phone connection in my apartment. So, what—what—
—what—what size apar—what—what—what size are you looking for?
Well, I just wanna make sure it’s gonna be compatible. I do a lotta hopscotch. Uh, so I wanna make sur—

You know, ya hop on the ground, left—left, right, left, right—right, left, right? Lotta hoppin’ up and down with hopscotch. I wanna make sure we’re gonna be compatible with the room and the flooring and the layout—
—No, I— [huffs] You—you’re gonna jump up—up and down on top of the other tenants’ heads. It—they’ll be annoyed! [sharp inhale & exhale]
Well maybe I could be under them.
—Are you looking for a two-bedroom apartment, a three-bedroom apartment, a rooming house room, a one-bedroom, a studio? [sharp inhale & exhale]
I’m open! I tend to set, uh, lotta booby traps—ya know, for intruders and stuff—so I wanna make sure it’s gonna be a good fit, ya know, compatability-wise. So I can set my booby traps—

—Oh, you—you—I—I—I’m—[sharp inhale]—you know, we don’t have—we’re—we’re filled up right now. Um, anything that we have is gonna be available down the line and I don’t know when we’re gonna be finished with the renovations.
Renovations? Well I don’t need anything that fancy! Um, I also house a lot of—
Hi! Yeah, it’s Buster. D’you think we could talk about, like, a shared rooming kinda thing? Instead?—
—I don’t have anything available that would work for you.
It—it sounds like you’re gonna bother the neighbors. I don’t have any ground floor units. You’re gonna bother the neighbors. [inhales]
—Um, you’ve got some sorta—I—and I don’t want my properties booby trapped. I—I’m sorry. You need to look elsewhere. I’m unable to assist you—
—Well my main thing is that I house a lot of jumping beans in there. That’s what I do for work. So, as long as we’re okay on that, I think we can prolly move forward!
I think that you’re—you’re—you’re—you’re—you’re playing games with me. And please lose my number—
Well, you should go to a shelter. I’m not a social service organization.
I have the money so we can get this put together—
—I don’t have any ground floor units avail—[inhales]—wha—what part of this don’t you understand? I do not have any ground floor units available!—
—It doesn’t have to be ground floor! I can be on a higher floor! No problem! All day!—
—Jumping on top of peoples’ heads! Jumping on top of my—you’ve already said that you jump and down and shuffle left and right. [unclear] the tenant that lives beneath you—
—I could give you some jumping beans free o’ charge!—

—According to New York state law, I can only accept one month’s security! I’m not interested in having you as a tenant. You’ve said that you jump up and down, you play hopscotch in your apartment, you booby trap your apartment—

—And it’s not about money! It’s about creating successful communities in which people can live. And Buster! I think you’re bustin’ my chops! I’m not interested in having you as a tenant. If you wanna file a complaint with me, go ahead!
A complaint? I don’t even know you—
—I have no ground floor units for me! Do not call me again!
Ah—what if I set a booby trap for you free o’ charge? Protect yourself?
—Don’t call me again—I’m not interested in renting to you. I’m not interested in carrying on any more conversations with you. Please leave me alone—
—Look, I’m gonna send ya a PayPal payment, okay? And we can just get things started that way. That’s no problem at all!
I don’t have a PayPal account.
Or Venmo. You know, whatever it takes.
Fuck you! Get out of my life!
Listen, I just need a place to live, here!
Hi! Yes, this is Buster, uh, Cheeks. I did wanna let you know I’m just driving—
—Oh, fuck you! Get outta my life.
I’m just gonna bring you some references, okay?
